requiredNextSteps: Provider review and protocol sign-off required before dispensing or administration.
Complete baseline labs before final protocol approval.
Schedule in-clinic onboarding and first-dose observation (if indicated).

fulfillment.reason: Patient preference (clinic) is eligible for glp1 track.

tiers.title: GLP-1 Starter
protocol: Provider-selected start: semaglutide 0.25 mg weekly OR tirzepatide 2.5 mg weekly.
Pulse schedule: 7-day dosing cadence with GI symptom check.
Primary: appetite regulation, craving control, early waist reduction.
tiers.bestFor: New GLP-1 patients needing close ramp-up support

tiers.title: GLP-1 Transform
protocol: Escalation: semaglutide 0.5-1.0 mg or tirzepatide 5-7.5 mg weekly as tolerated.
Primary: steady fat-mass reduction, improved fasting glucose.

tiers.title: GLP-1 Elite
protocol: Advanced range: semaglutide 1.7-2.4 mg or tirzepatide 10-15 mg weekly.
Monthly optimization cycle with rescue pathways.
Primary: high-velocity recomposition and maintenance architecture.
tiers.bestFor: High-touch patients targeting fastest safe results

providerHandoff.summary: Smoke B: GLP1 track, clinic fulfillment, goals=weight-loss, body-recomposition, metabolic-health, symptoms=appetite-dysregulation, sugar-cravings, weight-plateau.
dosingFramework: Start low: semaglutide 0.25 mg or tirzepatide 2.5 mg weekly.
Escalation pulse: 4-week intervals with hold/de-escalate for GI symptoms.
Dose decision uses appetite + adherence + adverse event composite.
monitoringChecklist: Required baseline labs ordered/reviewed before progression
2-4 week follow-up booked before protocol launch
Patient education confirmed (administration, side effects, red flags)
contraindicationNotes: No hard contraindication flags from intake; provider confirmation still required.

rationale: Baseline labs not yet completed — conservative entry tier applies.

dosageFramework.startRange: Mid-range GLP-1 protocol reference (e.g., semaglutide 0.5–1.0 mg or tirzepatide 5–7.5 mg weekly range). Agent selection and dose determination require provider authorization.
dosageFramework.cadence: Weekly subcutaneous protocol; maintain consistent schedule. Dose hold/escalation decision every 4 weeks with provider.
escalationCriteria: Suboptimal metabolic response after 4-week hold at current range — provider review required.
Written step-up order from prescribing provider required before range increase.
holdRules: GI symptoms requiring medical management — hold pending provider direction.
Abnormal liver enzymes (> 3× ULN) — hold and provider notification required.
Sustained heart rate increase > 20 bpm — hold and provider notification required.
Pregnancy confirmed — protocol hold required; provider to reassess entire program.
monitoringCadence: Monthly: weight trend, appetite score, adherence review.
Month 2: Provider visit; metabolic labs and optimization review.
Month 3: Comprehensive panel — A1c, lipids, CMP, thyroid if indicated.
dosageFramework.providerAuthorizationNote: PROVIDER AUTHORIZATION REQUIRED: This dosage framework is a clinical reference only. No protocol may be initiated, modified, or continued without explicit provider review and authorization. Provider is solely responsible for all prescribing decisions.
